evals.name: smoke-wiki-page-with-mermaid-and-code
evals.prompt: I need an Azure DevOps wiki page for our release checklist. Include a TOC, a Bash code block, and a Mermaid diagram that shows draft -> review -> publish.
evals.expected_output: Produces wiki-safe Markdown using [[_TOC_]], a fenced Bash code block, and a ::: mermaid block that uses graph syntax.

assertions.text: Response includes [[_TOC_]] and a ::: mermaid block
Response uses wiki-safe Mermaid syntax rather than ```mermaid fences

evals.name: edge-case-table-breaks-and-timeline
evals.prompt: My Azure DevOps wiki table needs a forced line break inside one cell, and I also want a timeline diagram for milestones. Show the correct syntax and call out any Azure-specific caveats.
evals.expected_output:
Explains that
works in wiki table cells, uses a timeline Mermaid block, and mentions wiki-specific limitations.

assertions.text:
Response mentions
for a wiki table cell
Response mentions Mermaid limitations or wiki-only scope

evals.name: negative-github-readme-request
evals.prompt: Write a GitHub README with a ```mermaid code fence and generic GitHub-flavored Markdown tables. This is not for Azure DevOps.
evals.expected_output: Does not force Azure DevOps wiki-only syntax onto a GitHub-only request and makes clear that the skill is Azure DevOps-specific.

assertions.text: Does not insist on Azure DevOps wiki syntax for a GitHub-only task

evals.name: disclosure-code-language-alias
evals.prompt: Which language tag should I use for a TypeScript code fence in an Azure DevOps wiki, and how can I verify it locally?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the code-language reference, recommends ts or typescript, and mentions the helper script.

evals.name: smoke-proposal-decision-tree
evals.prompt: Create an Azure DevOps wiki snippet that developers can use when proposing a rollout plan. Include a Mermaid decision tree and short sections for recommendation, risks, and next step.
evals.expected_output: Produces wiki-safe Markdown using a ::: mermaid block with graph syntax and follows the decision-tree proposal pattern.

assertions.text: Response includes a ::: mermaid block that uses graph TD or graph LR
Response includes recommendation, risks, and next step sections outside the diagram

evals.name: disclosure-advanced-wiki-features
evals.prompt: I want an Azure DevOps wiki page that shows child pages, wraps old content in a collapsible section, embeds an Azure Boards query, and mentions the owning group.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the advanced-features reference, uses [[_TOSP_]], <details>, query-table syntax, and distinguishes alias mentions from identity-guid mentions.

evals.name: support-matrix-surface-check
evals.prompt: Does Mermaid, KaTeX, and task lists work the same way in Azure DevOps README files, pull requests, and wiki pages?
evals.expected_output: Uses the support matrix and does not overstate wiki-only features on README files.

assertions.text: Response distinguishes wiki-only features from PR or README support

evals.name: edge-ambiguous-highlight-alias
evals.prompt: Can I use ls as the code-fence language tag in Azure DevOps, or is that ambiguous?
evals.expected_output: Explains that ls is an ambiguous Highlight.js alias overlap and recommends a full language name or the helper script result.

assertions.text: Response mentions alias overlap or ambiguity
